PLNTF'S REPLY TO DEFNT'S OPPOS TO PLNTF'S MOTION FOR NEW HEARING REGARDING REQUEST 	; TO COMPEL DEFNT'S COMPLIANCE W/ THE COURT'S ORDER OF 10/15/04; REQUEST FOR TERMINATING EVIDENTIARY ISSUE OR MONETARY SANCTIONS FILED BY PLAINTIFF UZAN, AYLA ASHABOGLU, FEHMI
MEDIATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE OF MAR-23-2005 CONTINUED TO JUN-22-2005 AT 10:00 AM IN DEPT. 212. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

DISCOVERY 612, APPEARANCES: PETER R. SOARES, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY, ALLSTATE INSURANCE COMPANY. SCOTT A. BROWN, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF/OPPOSING PARTY. HEARING HELD. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. AS TO THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS. 6, 7, 12-15, 18, 19, 23-25, 28, AND 32, THE MOTION IS GRANTED. THE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED AND PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO RESPOND FURTHER WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. THE RESPONSES SHALL BE COMPLETE AT THE TIME AT WHICH THEY ARE MADE. NONE OF THE INTERROGATORIES INVADE WORK PRODUCT; AND AS THEY SEEK CONTENTIONS, THE COURTS HAVE HELD THAT THEY MUST BE ANSWERED. AS TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NOS. 21 AND 22, THE MOTION IS GRANTED. THE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED EXCEPT FOR CLAIMS OF PRIVILEGE. ANY DOCUMENTS WITHHELD OR PORTIONS OF DOCUMENTS REDACTED BASED UPON A CLAIM OF PRIVILEGE, SHALL BE IDENTIFIED WITH SPECIFICITY IN A PRIVILEGE LOG. THE DOCUMENTS AND PRIVILEGE LOG SHALL BE PRODUCED WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. DEFENDANT'S EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE COURT'S ORDER OF MARCH 3, 2005; OR EXTENDING THE TIME BY WHICH ALLSTATE MUST COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S MARCH 3, 2005 DISCOVERY ORDER IS OFF CALENDAR AT THE REQUEST OF THE MOVING PARTY. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

DISCOVERY 612, PRESENT: DAVID R. SIMONTON, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY, ALLSTATE INSURANCE COMPANY. HEARING HELD. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR NEW HEARING REGARDING REQUEST NO. 9 OF PLAINTIFF'S DOCUMENT DEMAND AND TO COMPEL DEFENDANT'S COMPLIANCE WITH THE COURT'S ORDER OF OCTOBER 15, 2004 IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO REQUEST NO. 9, LIMITED TO WRITINGS, CONSISTING OF BROCHURES, MEMORANDUMS, AND INFORMATIONAL LITERATURE REGARDING MOLD AND WATER DAMAGE COVERAGE. THE COURT PREVIOUSLY RULED THAT THE CLAIMS OF PROPRIETARY INFORMATION AND PRIVILEGE WERE NOT OVERRULED. WHILE THE COURT DID NOT PREVIOUSLY ADDRESS THE MATTER OF A CONFIDENTIALITY ORDER, AS IT WAS NOT RAISED BY DEFENDANT, THE COURT DOES FIND THAT THE ENTRY OF A CONFIDENTIALITY ORDER WOULD BE APPROPRIATE AND DOES APPROVE THE ENTRY OF SUCH AN ORDER. FURTHER PRODUCTION IN COMPLIANCE WITH THIS ORDER SHALL BE MADE WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. THE COURT DOES NOT FIND ANY BASIS FOR THE REQUESTED TERMINATING, EVIDENTIARY, ISSUE, AND/OR MONETARY SANCTIONS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-24-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Kenneth Litton, appeared for defendant/moving party
David R. Simonton, appeared for Defendant Allstate Insurance
David Poore, Atty. for Plaintiff/opposing party appeared via Telephone

Ruling:
Hearing held. Tentative Ruling adopted: Defendant Paul Davis Restoration's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production of Documents is granted, limited to the settlement agreement. Production shall be made pursuant to a confidentiality order, maintaining the confidentiality otherwise called for by the provisions of the agreement attached to Allstate Insurance Company's limited opposition filed on October 21, 2005. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set No. Two, And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-24-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Kenneth Litton, appeared for defendant/moving party
David R. Simonton, appeared for Defendant Allstate Insurance
David Poore, Atty. for Plaintiff/opposing party appeared via Telephone

Ruling:
Hearing held. Tentative Ruling adopted with modifications. Defendant Paul Davis Restoration's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set No. 2, is granted. Plaintiff's objections are overruled. While counsel argues in the Points and Authorities that the settlement was confidential, there is no evidence to support that argument. E.C. Section provides that a written settlement agreement is not protected from disclosure if the agreement is binding. There is no evidence to support a finding that the agreement is not binding. Plaintiff is ordered to respond within 10 days of notice of this order.
The court finds that the opposition was made without substantial justification. Sanctions are imposed against Plaintiff in the amount of $966.30, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order.

MODIFICATIONS:
Responses to be subject to confidentiality order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
